test.name: Read CSV file to list and add data to database

kw.name: Get File
kw.doc: Returns the contents of a specified file.

kw.name: Get File
kw.msg: ${outputRows} = ﻿Descirption;Quantity;Unit;UnitPrice;VATPercent;VAT;Total;InvoiceNumber;RowNumber Installation Services;5;h;60;24;72;372;143143;1 Replacement Products;10;kpl;105;24;252;1302;143143;2 Keyboard Logitech...
kw.doc: Returns the contents of a specified file.

kw.name: Log
kw.doc: Logs the given message with the given level.

kw.name: Log
kw.msg: ﻿Descirption;Quantity;Unit;UnitPrice;VATPercent;VAT;Total;InvoiceNumber;RowNumber Installation Services;5;h;60;24;72;372;143143;1 Replacement Products;10;kpl;105;24;252;1302;143143;2 Keyboard Logitech;10;kpl;40;24;96;496;1543235;1 Brother printer;1;kpl;65;24;15.6;80.6;1543235;2 Installation Services;5;h;60;24;72;372;153143;1 Replacement Products;10;kpl;105;24;252;1302;153143;2 Cleaning Services;5;h;60;24;72;372;153143;3 Cleaning Wipes;10;kpl;105;24;252;1302;153143;4
kw.doc: Logs the given message with the given level.

kw.name: Split String
kw.doc: Splits the ``string`` using ``separator`` as a delimiter string.

kw.name: Split String
kw.doc: Splits the ``string`` using ``separator`` as a delimiter string.

kw.name: Get Length
kw.doc: Returns and logs the length of the given item as an integer.

kw.name: Evaluate
kw.doc: Evaluates the given expression in Python and returns the result.

kw.name: Convert To Integer
kw.doc: Converts the given item to an integer number.

kw.name: Remove From List
kw.doc: Removes and returns the value specified with an ``index`` from ``list``.

kw.name: Remove From List
kw.doc: Removes and returns the value specified with an ``index`` from ``list``.

kw.name: Get Length
kw.doc: Returns and logs the length of the given item as an integer.

kw.name: Evaluate
kw.doc: Evaluates the given expression in Python and returns the result.

kw.name: Remove From List
kw.doc: Removes and returns the value specified with an ``index`` from ``list``.

kw.name: Remove From List
kw.doc: Removes and returns the value specified with an ``index`` from ``list``.

kw.name: Log
kw.doc: Logs the given message with the given level.

kw.name: Log
kw.msg: ﻿Descirption;Quantity;Unit;UnitPrice;VATPercent;VAT;Total;InvoiceNumber;RowNumber Installation Services;5;h;60;24;72;372;143143;1 Replacement Products;10;kpl;105;24;252;1302;143143;2 Keyboard Logitech;10;kpl;40;24;96;496;1543235;1 Brother printer;1;kpl;65;24;15.6;80.6;1543235;2 Installation Services;5;h;60;24;72;372;153143;1 Replacement Products;10;kpl;105;24;252;1302;153143;2 Cleaning Services;5;h;60;24;72;372;153143;3 Cleaning Wipes;10;kpl;105;24;252;1302;153143;4
kw.doc: Logs the given message with the given level.

kw.name: Log
kw.doc: Logs the given message with the given level.

kw.name: Split String
kw.doc: Splits the ``string`` using ``separator`` as a delimiter string.

kw.name: Connect To Database
kw.msg: Connect to DB using : pymysql.connect(db='rpakurssi', user='robotuser', passwd=***, host='localhost', port=3306, charset='utf8mb4')
kw.doc: Creates a database connection using the DB API 2.0 ``db_module`` and the parameters provided. Along with listed commonly used arguments (`db_name`, `db_host` etc.) you can set any other DB module specific parameters as key/value pairs.

kw.name: Convert Date
kw.doc: Converts between supported `date formats`.

kw.name: Convert Date
kw.doc: Converts between supported `date formats`.

kw.name: Set Variable
kw.msg: ${insertStmt} = insert into invoiceheader (invoiceNumber, companyname, referencenumber, invoicedate, duedate, companycode, bankaccountnumber, amountexclvat, vat, totalamount, invoicestatus_id, comments) values ('1431...
kw.arg: insert into invoiceheader (invoiceNumber, companyname, referencenumber, invoicedate, duedate, companycode, bankaccountnumber, amountexclvat, vat, totalamount, invoicestatus_id, comments) values ('${items}[0]', '${items}[1]', '${items}[2]', '${invoicedate}', '${duedate}', '${items}[5]', '${items}[6]', ${items}[7], ${items}[8], ${items}[9], -1, 'Processing');
kw.doc: Returns the given values which can then be assigned to a variables.

kw.name: Log
kw.msg: insert into invoiceheader (invoiceNumber, companyname, referencenumber, invoicedate, duedate, companycode, bankaccountnumber, amountexclvat, vat, totalamount, invoicestatus_id, comments) values ('143143', 'Service Provider Oy', '1431432', '2022.12.15', '2021.04.07', '1234567-8', 'FI12 3456 7890 1234 56', 1350, 324, 1674, -1, 'Processing');
kw.doc: Logs the given message with the given level.

kw.name: Execute Sql String
kw.msg:
Executing sql:
insert into invoiceheader (invoiceNumber, companyname, referencenumber, invoicedate, duedate, companycode, bankaccountnumber, amountexclvat, vat, totalamount, invoicestatus_id, comments) values ('143143', 'Service Provider Oy', '1431432', '2022.12.15', '2021.04.07', '1234567-8', 'FI12 3456 7890 1234 56', 1350, 324, 1674, -1, 'Processing');
kw.doc: Executes the ``sql_string`` as a single SQL command.

kw.name: Disconnect From Database
kw.doc: Disconnects from the database.

kw.name: Log
kw.doc: Logs the given message with the given level.

kw.name: Split String
kw.doc: Splits the ``string`` using ``separator`` as a delimiter string.

kw.name: Connect To Database
kw.msg: Connect to DB using : pymysql.connect(db='rpakurssi', user='robotuser', passwd=***, host='localhost', port=3306, charset='utf8mb4')
kw.doc: Creates a database connection using the DB API 2.0 ``db_module`` and the parameters provided. Along with listed commonly used arguments (`db_name`, `db_host` etc.) you can set any other DB module specific parameters as key/value pairs.

kw.doc: Converts between supported `date formats`.

kw.doc: Logs the given message with the given level.

kw.name: Execute Sql String
kw.msg:
Executing sql:

kw.doc: Executes the ``sql_string`` as a single SQL command.

kw.name: Set Variable
kw.msg: ${insertStmt} = insert ignore into invoicerow (invoicenumber, invoicerownumber, description, quantity, unit, unitprice, vatpercent, vat, total) values ('143143', '1', 'Installation Services', '5', 'h', '60', '24', '7...
kw.arg: insert ignore into invoicerow (invoicenumber, invoicerownumber, description, quantity, unit, unitprice, vatpercent, vat, total) values ('${items}[7]', '${items}[8]', '${items}[0]', '${items}[1]', '${items}[2]', '${items}[3]', '${items}[4]', '${items}[5]', '${items}[6]');
kw.doc: Returns the given values which can then be assigned to a variables.

kw.name: Log
kw.msg: insert ignore into invoicerow (invoicenumber, invoicerownumber, description, quantity, unit, unitprice, vatpercent, vat, total) values ('143143', '1', 'Installation Services', '5', 'h', '60', '24', '72', '372');
kw.doc: Logs the given message with the given level.

kw.name: Execute Sql String
kw.msg:
Executing sql:

kw.msg:
Executing sql:
insert ignore into invoicerow (invoicenumber, invoicerownumber, description, quantity, unit, unitprice, vatpercent, vat, total) values ('153143', '4', 'Cleaning Wipes', '10', 'kpl', '105', '24', '252', '1302');
kw.doc: Executes the ``sql_string`` as a single SQL command.

kw.name: Disconnect From Database
kw.doc: Disconnects from the database.

test.name: Validate and update validation info to db

kw.name: Connect To Database
kw.msg: Connect to DB using : pymysql.connect(db='rpakurssi', user='robotuser', passwd=***, host='localhost', port=3306, charset='utf8mb4')
kw.doc: Creates a database connection using the DB API 2.0 ``db_module`` and the parameters provided. Along with listed commonly used arguments (`db_name`, `db_host` etc.) you can set any other DB module specific parameters as key/value pairs.

kw.name: Query
kw.msg:
Executing sql:
select invoicenumber, referencenumber, bankaccountnumber, totalamount from invoiceheader where invoicestatus_id=-1;

Query returned 3 rows
Rowinvoicenumberreferencenumberbankaccountnumbertotalamount
01431431431432FI12 3456 7890 1234 561674
11531431531439FI12 3456 7890 1234 563348
2154323515432359FI05 1234 5600 7891 01577
kw.arg: select invoicenumber, referencenumber, bankaccountnumber, totalamount from invoiceheader where invoicestatus_id=-1;
kw.doc: Runs a query with the ``select_statement`` and returns the result as list of rows. The type of row values depends on the database module - usually they are tuples or tuple-like objects.

kw.name: Log
kw.doc: Logs the given message with the given level.

kw.name: Log
kw.doc: Logs the given message with the given level.

kw.name: Set Variable
kw.doc: Returns the given values which can then be assigned to a variables.

kw.name: Set Variable
kw.doc: Returns the given values which can then be assigned to a variables.

kw.name: Run Keyword And Return Status
Evaluate
kw.doc: Evaluates the given expression in Python and returns the result.

kw.name: Should Be Equal As Numbers
kw.doc: Fails if objects are unequal after converting them to real numbers.
Runs the given keyword with given arguments and returns the status as a Boolean value.

kw.name: Run Keyword If
kw.doc: Runs the given keyword with the given arguments, if ``condition`` is true.

kw.name: Run Keyword If
kw.doc: Runs the given keyword with the given arguments, if ``condition`` is true.

kw.name: Run Keyword And Return Status
Evaluate
kw.doc: Evaluates the given expression in Python and returns the result.

kw.name: Get Length
kw.doc: Returns and logs the length of the given item as an integer.

kw.name: Should Be True
kw.doc: Fails if the given condition is not true.

kw.name: Evaluate
kw.doc: Evaluates the given expression in Python and returns the result.

kw.name: Evaluate
kw.arg: ''.join([str(ord(c) - 55) if c.isalpha() else c for c in '${rearranged}'])
kw.doc: Evaluates the given expression in Python and returns the result.

kw.doc: Evaluates the given expression in Python and returns the result.

kw.name: Create List
kw.doc: Returns a list containing given items.

kw.name: Set Variable
kw.msg: ${updateStmt} = update invoiceheader set invoicestatus_id=%s, comments = %s where invoicenumber = %s;
kw.arg: update invoiceheader set invoicestatus_id=%s, comments = %s where invoicenumber = %s;
kw.doc: Returns the given values which can then be assigned to a variables.

kw.name: Execute Sql String
kw.msg:
Executing sql:
update invoiceheader set invoicestatus_id=%s, comments = %s where invoicenumber = %s;
Parameters: ('0', 'all ok', 143143)
kw.doc: Executes the ``sql_string`` as a single SQL command.
